prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_payment_instruction origin_leg_1)
(register_payment_instruction beneficiary_leg_1)
(register_payment_instruction processing_work_unit_1)
(register_payment_instruction processing_work_unit_2)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_instruction origin_leg_1 settlement_channel_1)
(assign_message_endpoint_to_instruction origin_leg_1 message_endpoint_1)
(release_payment_instruction origin_leg_1 message_endpoint_1)
(evaluate_origin_leg_channel origin_leg_1 origin_execution_channel_1 message_endpoint_1)
(assign_approver_to_instruction origin_leg_1 approver_1)
(authorize_origin_leg_for_routing origin_leg_1 origin_execution_channel_1 approver_1)
(release_approver_assignment origin_leg_1 approver_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_instruction beneficiary_leg_1 settlement_channel_2)
(assign_message_endpoint_to_instruction beneficiary_leg_1 message_endpoint_1)
(release_payment_instruction beneficiary_leg_1 message_endpoint_1)
(evaluate_beneficiary_leg_channel beneficiary_leg_1 beneficiary_execution_channel_1 message_endpoint_1)
(assign_approver_to_instruction beneficiary_leg_1 approver_1)
(authorize_beneficiary_leg_for_routing beneficiary_leg_1 beneficiary_execution_channel_1 approver_1)
(release_approver_assignment beneficiary_leg_1 approver_1)
(create_settlement_work_record origin_leg_1 beneficiary_leg_1 origin_execution_channel_1 beneficiary_execution_channel_1 settlement_work_record_1)
(confirm_work_record_ready_for_document_check settlement_work_record_1 origin_leg_1 message_endpoint_1)
(finalize_origin_leg origin_leg_1 settlement_work_record_1)
(finalize_beneficiary_leg beneficiary_leg_1 settlement_work_record_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_instruction processing_work_unit_1 settlement_channel_3)
(assign_message_endpoint_to_instruction processing_work_unit_1 message_endpoint_1)
(release_payment_instruction processing_work_unit_1 message_endpoint_1)
(attach_compliance_reference_to_work_record processing_work_unit_1 compliance_reference_1 settlement_work_record_1)
(claim_work_unit_for_operator_handling processing_work_unit_1 compliance_reference_1 settlement_work_record_1 message_endpoint_1)
(assign_execution_mode_to_work_unit processing_work_unit_1 execution_mode_token_1)
(assign_escalation_level_to_work_unit processing_work_unit_1 escalation_level_1)
(assign_approver_to_instruction processing_work_unit_1 approver_1)
(operator_claim_work_unit_for_execution processing_work_unit_1 execution_mode_token_1 approver_1 compliance_reference_1 settlement_work_record_1)
(release_approver_assignment processing_work_unit_1 approver_1)
(operator_apply_escalation_level processing_work_unit_1 escalation_level_1 compliance_reference_1 settlement_work_record_1)
(mark_work_unit_ready_for_finalization processing_work_unit_1)
(revoke_execution_mode_from_work_unit processing_work_unit_1 execution_mode_token_1)
(revoke_escalation_level_from_work_unit processing_work_unit_1 escalation_level_1)
(initiate_instruction_finalization processing_work_unit_1 escalation_reason_token_1 message_endpoint_1)
(complete_work_unit_settlement processing_work_unit_1 settlement_channel_3 escalation_reason_token_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_instruction processing_work_unit_2 settlement_channel_3)
(assign_message_endpoint_to_instruction processing_work_unit_2 message_endpoint_1)
(release_payment_instruction processing_work_unit_2 message_endpoint_1)
(attach_compliance_reference_to_work_record processing_work_unit_2 compliance_reference_2 settlement_work_record_1)
(claim_work_unit_for_operator_handling processing_work_unit_2 compliance_reference_2 settlement_work_record_1 message_endpoint_1)
(assign_execution_mode_to_work_unit processing_work_unit_2 execution_mode_token_1)
(assign_escalation_level_to_work_unit processing_work_unit_2 escalation_level_1)
(assign_approver_to_instruction processing_work_unit_2 approver_1)
(operator_claim_work_unit_for_execution processing_work_unit_2 execution_mode_token_1 approver_1 compliance_reference_2 settlement_work_record_1)
(release_approver_assignment processing_work_unit_2 approver_1)
(operator_apply_escalation_level processing_work_unit_2 escalation_level_1 compliance_reference_2 settlement_work_record_1)
(mark_work_unit_ready_for_finalization processing_work_unit_2)
(revoke_execution_mode_from_work_unit processing_work_unit_2 execution_mode_token_1)
(revoke_escalation_level_from_work_unit_2 escalation_level_1)
(initiate_instruction_finalization processing_work_unit_2 escalation_reason_token_1 message_endpoint_1)
(complete_work_unit_settlement processing_work_unit_2 settlement_channel_3 escalation_reason_token_1)
(initiate_instruction_finalization origin_leg_1 escalation_reason_token_1 message_endpoint_1)
(complete_origin_leg_settlement origin_leg_1 settlement_channel_1 escalation_reason_token_1)
(initiate_instruction_finalization beneficiary_leg_1 escalation_reason_token_1 message_endpoint_1)
(complete_beneficiary_leg_settlement beneficiary_leg_1 settlement_channel_2 escalation_reason_token_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, but I need to find a plan to achieve the goal: settle origin_leg_1, beneficiary_leg_1, processing_work_unit_1, and processing_work_unit_2.


## Assessing the Problem  
- The task involves settling origin_leg_1, beneficiary_leg_1, processing_work_unit_1, and processing_work_unit_2, which seems complex and interconnected.  
- A clear plan is needed to address each component systematically while understanding their relationships.  

## Understanding Initial Conditions  
- First, examine the initial state to identify the current setup of origin_leg_1 and beneficiary_leg_1.  
- Also, review processing_work_unit_1 and processing_work_unit_2 to determine their roles and dependencies.

## Investigating Settlement Actions  
- Settlement of origin_leg_1 and beneficiary_leg_1 requires finalization_in_progress, assigned channels, and escalation reasons.  
- For processing_work_unit_1 and processing_work_unit_2 (pu1, pu2), finalization involves linking to the same settlement_work_record_1 and addressing authorization profiles.  

## Examining Authorization Profiles  
- Only authorization_profile_1 is initially available, while pu2 depends on authorization_profile_2, which isn’t ready, potentially requiring special handling.  
- This may lead to exception escalation, complicating the settlement process for pu2 until auth2 is available.  

## Planning Initial Steps  
- Start by registering all components as payment instructions, like register_payment_instruction for origin_leg_1.  
- Then, assign settlement channels and endpoints, ensuring they’re released for processing to move forward with settlement actions.  

## Analyzing Dependencies  
- Legs and work units need specific actions like evaluate_origin_leg_channel, which require released_for_processing and assigned endpoints.  
- Creating settlement_work_record needs assessed legs, linked documents, and readiness, adding layers to the settlement process.

## Outlining the Plan  
- Start by processing origin_leg_1 and beneficiary_leg_1 first, as creating settlement_work_record depends on them being assessed and ready.  
- Register each leg, assign channels and endpoints, and release them for processing to prepare for further actions.  

## Processing Legs Individually  
- For origin_leg_1, follow steps like evaluating the channel with message_endpoint_1 and assigning approver_1 to make it assessed and ready.  
- Similarly, process beneficiary_leg_1, potentially using different channels but sharing message_endpoint_1, ensuring no conflicts in endpoint assignment.  

## Handling Work Record Creation  
- After legs are ready, create settlement_work_record_1, which is necessary for attaching compliance references to processing_work_unit_1 and processing_work_unit_2.  
- Ensure work_record_1 is ready for document check, linking both pu1 and pu2 to the same record for unified settlement.
Given the ExistingSummary and the NewReasoningContent, I'll extend the flow with new reasoning steps, ensuring continuity and aligning with the previous themes. The last subheading in ExistingSummary is "Handling Work Record Creation," so I'll start by extending that topic since the NewReasoningContent continues the process of handling work units and settlement.

## Handling Work Record Creation
- Once settlement_work_record_1 is created without requires flags, work units like pu1 and pu2 can move forward without additional document requirements.
- Claiming work units for operator handling is possible if they’re released, have compliance references, and are linked to the work record, with no beneficiary document requirements.

## Preparing Work Units for Claiming
- Assign execution mode and escalation level to work units, ensuring tokens and levels are available and shared across pu1 and pu2.
- Assign approvers to work units after releasing previous assignments, preparing for operator claiming with shared resources like message_endpoint_1.

## Executing Work Unit Claims
- Operator claims work units like pu1 when ready, requiring execution mode, approver, and compliance linkage, with no beneficiary requirements.
- After claiming, applying escalation levels processes the work unit, setting it up for finalization without needing priority assignment.

## Finalizing Settlement Process
- Mark work units ready for finalization after processing, as they don’t require priority and meet readiness criteria.
- Initiate finalization for settlement once work units are ready, completing the process without additional priority steps.

## Handling Work Record Creation  
- Work units like pu1 and pu2 need compliance references linked to work records, set during processing, for operator claims and escalation.  
- Work records are initially linked during setup, ensuring no additional linking is needed for processing.

## Preparing Work Units for Claiming  
- Release approver assignments for legs and payment instructions after authorization, ensuring flexibility for subsequent operator claims.  
- Assign execution modes and escalation levels before claiming, as they’re required for operator processing, with shared tokens across units.

## Managing Settlement Channels  
- Only three settlement channels are available (settlement_channel_1, 2, 3), but four items (origin, beneficiary, pu1, pu2) need assignment, creating a channel availability conflict.  
- Channels become unavailable after assignment but can be reused after settlement completion, though initial assignment requires all to be selected early.

## Processing Work Unit Settlements  
- After pu1 is ready for finalization, initiate and complete its settlement using sc3 and token1, making both available again.  
- This allows resources like sc3 and token1 to be reused for processing pu2, ensuring efficient resource management.  

## Managing Resource Availability  
- Revoke execution mode (token1) and escalation level (level1) from pu1 after settlement to free resources for pu2.  
- Release approver assignments for pu1 post-claim, ensuring availability for subsequent work units like pu2.  

## Preparing pu2 for Processing  
- Register and assign settlement channel sc3 to pu2 once available, then attach compliance reference comp2 to wr1 for readiness.  
- Assign message endpoint me1 and release pu2, followed by claiming and applying escalation level to process pu2 to readiness.  

## Finalizing All Settlements  
- Mark pu2 ready for finalization and settle using available token1 and sc3, completing its processing.  
- Settle origin_leg_1 and benef_leg_1 using sc1 and sc2 with token1, ensuring all legs are finalized without unassigning channels.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
